items.text:
BIOL 215: Introduction to Marine Ecology
Instructor: Dr. Marisol Chen | Office: Halloran Science Building, Room 312
Office Hours: Tuesdays and Thursdays, 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM

This course meets Mondays and Wednesdays from 10:00 AM to 11:30 AM in Room 118. Students must complete all assigned readings before each session, as class discussions assume familiarity with the material.

Grading is divided as follows:
- Two midterm exams: 40% combined
- Final research project: 30%
- Weekly laboratory reports: 20%
- Participation: 10%

Laboratory reports are due every Friday by 5:00 PM through the course portal. Late reports will be accepted for up to 48 hours, but a $2.00 processing penalty is deducted from each late submission. Reports submitted after that window receive no credit.

The final research project requires a field trip to Coverly Tidal Reserve on October 18. A $15 transportation fee must be paid by October 4. Students who cannot attend should email Dr. Chen to arrange an alternative assignment.

For accommodations, please contact the Student Access Office before the second week of the semester.

questions.stem: According to the syllabus, what happens when a laboratory report is submitted within 48 hours after the deadline?

options.A: A revised version must be resubmitted through the portal
options.B: A new due date is assigned by the instructor directly
options.C: A two-dollar penalty is deducted from the submission
options.D: A reduction of one full letter grade is applied

questions.explanation: The syllabus states that late reports are accepted for up to 48 hours but a $2.00 processing penalty is deducted from each late submission.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who are unable to join the field trip on October 18?

options.A: They may receive a different task arranged with the instructor
options.B: They will forfeit the entire thirty percent project grade
options.C: They must repeat the laboratory component the next semester
options.D: They should request a refund of the transportation fee online

questions.explanation: The syllabus says students who cannot attend should email Dr. Chen to arrange an alternative assignment, implying a substitute task is available.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this syllabus?

options.A: To summarize the history of marine ecology research methods
options.B: To advertise an upcoming field trip to a tidal reserve
options.C: To compare grading systems used across science departments
options.D: To outline the course requirements, grading, and key deadlines

questions.explanation: The document presents the meeting times, grading breakdown, submission rules, and important dates, which together describe the course requirements.

items.text:
The Quad Cafe — Weekly Lunch Menu
Served Monday through Friday, 11:00 AM to 2:30 PM, in the Student Union basement.

All meals include a side salad and a beverage. Prices are listed below:
- Grilled vegetable wrap: $6.50
- Roasted chicken bowl: $7.75
- Black bean chili (vegan): $5.25
- Baked salmon plate: $9.00

Daily specials rotate each weekday. On Mondays, soup of the day is $3.00 with any entree. On Wednesdays, students who show a valid library card receive a free cookie with their purchase. Friday features a $4.00 build-your-own pasta station from 12:00 PM to 1:30 PM only.

Meal-plan holders may use their card for one entree per visit at no additional charge. Guests without a plan must pay the listed prices in cash or by card. A $1.50 surcharge applies to all orders placed after 2:00 PM.

Reusable containers are available for a $5.00 refundable deposit, returned when the container is brought back clean. To reserve a large table for a group of eight or more, please email quadcafe@campus.edu at least one day in advance.

questions.stem: According to the menu, what do students need to do to receive a free cookie?

options.A: Order the build-your-own pasta on a Friday afternoon
options.B: Buy a soup of the day along with any listed entree
options.C: Show a valid library card on a Wednesday purchase
options.D: Return a reusable container clean for the deposit refund

questions.explanation: The menu states that on Wednesdays, students who show a valid library card receive a free cookie with their purchase.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a guest who orders the baked salmon plate at 2:15 PM?

options.A: They receive a complimentary side salad and beverage included
options.B: They pay a total of ten dollars and fifty cents for the order
options.C: They must reserve a large table by emailing the cafe first
options.D: They are charged the full price plus an added late surcharge

questions.explanation: The salmon plate is $9.00, and a $1.50 surcharge applies to all orders placed after 2:00 PM, so a 2:15 PM order includes the surcharge.

questions.stem: According to the menu, what must a group do to reserve a large table?

options.A: To email the cafe at least one day ahead of time
options.B: To pay a refundable deposit of five dollars per person
options.C: To arrive before the lunch service opens each morning
options.D: To purchase a daily special for every member present

questions.explanation: The menu says that to reserve a large table for a group of eight or more, you should email quadcafe@campus.edu at least one day in advance.
